panels.action: Rani Lakshmibai of Jhansi rides a rearing horse across a dusty Bundelkhand plain, talwar raised and eyes blazing with resolve, Gwalior’s fort silhouetted on a distant hill under a hot mid-19th-century sky.

Rani Lakshmibai stands on a rocky rise overlooking scattered tents and weary riders, her horse beside her, wind tugging at her Maratha warrior dress as smoke from distant Gwalior drifts on the horizon.

dialogue.text: After Jhansi fell, the Rani rode on.

dialogue.speaker: rani_lakshmibai_of_jhansi
dialogue.text: Jhansi is lost, but our duty is not.

panels.action: From behind Rani Lakshmibai, we see her pointing her talwar toward the distant Gwalior fort while unseen comrades’ hands tighten on reins and spear shafts at the edge of frame, dust swirling around their horses.

dialogue.speaker: rani_lakshmibai_of_jhansi
dialogue.text: We ride for Gwalior! For Hindustan!

panels.action: Rani Lakshmibai gallops at the head of a cavalry column along a dusty road toward Kotah-ki-Serai, her braid streaming behind her as hooves kick up clouds and the mid-19th-century Bundelkhand countryside blurs with speed-lines.

dialogue.text: Near Kotah-ki-Serai, enemy cavalry closed in.

dialogue.speaker: rani_lakshmibai_of_jhansi
dialogue.text: If we break here, hope breaks too.
